frameworks.name: two-streams (dorsal/ventral) hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: extends classical division by giving dorsal cortex a constitutive role in object recognition

frameworks.name: structural / part-based object recognition
frameworks.one_line: objects are recognized via spatial relations among parts forming global shape

frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis of cortical representations
frameworks.one_line: decoding and effective connectivity used to read out representational content

core_question: Does dorsal visual cortex compute object-centered spatial relations among parts and feed them to ventral cortex for object recognition?
key_assumptions: global shape perception requires representing spatial relations among object parts
task-dependent connectivity reflects functional information transfer between dorsal and ventral cortex
intraparietal sulcus can be functionally parcellated by the computation it supports
manipulations: object-centered part relations vs other dorsal computations (allocentric relations, 3D shape, tools)
decoding object category from posterior IPS vs ventral object regions
key_findings: IPS regions selectively engaged in computing object-centered part relations were identified
these regions showed task-dependent functional and effective connectivity with ventral cortex
posterior IPS multivariate response decoded object category at levels comparable to ventral object regions
mediation and effective connectivity suggested IPS contributes to ventral part-relation representations
interpretations: dorsal cortex computes object-centered part relations and transmits them to ventral pathway to support categorization
object recognition is a joint dorsal-ventral process rather than a purely ventral one
